### Hypercare Plan

Plan defining the hypercare phase following a Teams Phone cutover.

#### Purpose

Stabilize the migrated Teams Phone scope after go-live by increasing monitoring, shortening triage loops and ensuring that issues are transferred cleanly to RUN.

#### Focus areas

- enhanced monitoring
- priority incident handling
- daily follow-up with delivery and RUN stakeholders
- validation of recurring call-flow symptoms
- user feedback triage
- evidence review
- known-issue tracking

#### Entry criteria

- cutover completed or accepted with known limitations
- validation results captured
- support contacts confirmed
- escalation boundaries communicated
- known issues documented

#### Hypercare activities

- monitor critical user and service-entry call paths
- review incident tickets daily
- classify issues by tenant, endpoint, network, operator or user adoption
- verify Auto Attendant and Call Queue behavior under real usage
- track fixes, workarounds and residual risks
- prepare transition to steady-state support

#### Exit criteria

- no unresolved critical incidents
- known issues have owners and target dates
- support has handover information
- evidence and validation outputs are archived
- business owner accepts transition to RUN

#### Evidence expectations

- daily issue log
- incident classification summary
- validation rerun results if required
- known issue register
- support handover confirmation

#### Acceptance criteria

- support can operate the service without build-team dependency
- critical issues are closed or owned
- recurring symptoms are documented
- hypercare exit is explicit, not assumed
